name: Static Promotion (PMID) created, with lifecycle metadata
description: SFCC OCAPI Data API response to PUT .../promotions/SFloyalty-20OFFLEGO. Defines the discount logic. searchable=true enables Search-Show?pmid= retrieval. Native start_date/end_date let B2C disable it at expiry with no code. Custom attrs (isLoyaltyManaged / loyaltyStatus / loyaltyAutoCleanup) drive the housekeeping sweeps (section 5).
notes: searchable:true is required for the ?pmid= PLP retrieval. exclusivity 'no' allows stacking with other loyalty PMIDs. The c_ metadata is what Trigger 2 (cron sweep) filters on: purge where c_isLoyaltyManaged=true AND c_loyaltyAutoCleanup=true AND end_date older than N days. OCAPI REALITY: the DISCOUNT logic (the 20% off / qualifier rules) is NOT creatable via OCAPI Data, it's configured in Business Manager; the studio drives the storefront promotional_price (list*0.80) directly. A promotion's active window is classically governed by its campaign schedule, not the Promotion document; the native start_date/end_date here reflect the design's stated 'native expiry' intent alongside the c_loyaltyStatus lifecycle. What OCAPI Data legitimately round-trips on /promotions/{id} is id, name, callout_msg, enabled, searchable, exclusivity, promotion_class and the c_ attrs.
